question: The concept of 'human resource planning'
options: Introduces new employees to the organisation, their role and colleagues
Training delivered away from the workplace, often by external providers
Hygiene factors prevent dissatisfaction; motivators create satisfaction
Forecasting future staffing needs and planning how to meet them
answer: Forecasting future staffing needs and planning how to meet them

question: 'Recruitment' differs from 'selection' because
options: The rate at which employees leave and are replaced in an organisation
Managers must meet lower-level needs before higher-level motivators work
Training delivered away from the workplace, often by external providers
Recruitment attracts candidates; selection chooses the best one
answer: Recruitment attracts candidates; selection chooses the best one

question: The concept of 'induction training'
options: Training delivered away from the workplace, often by external providers
Managers must meet lower-level needs before higher-level motivators work
Introduces new employees to the organisation, their role and colleagues
Strikes, work-to-rule, go-slow and overtime bans
answer: Introduces new employees to the organisation, their role and colleagues

question: 'On-the-job training' advantages include
options: Evaluate and improve employee performance through structured feedback
Cost-effective and directly relevant to the actual work environment
The rate at which employees leave and are replaced in an organisation
Communication, supervision quality and employee autonomy
answer: Cost-effective and directly relevant to the actual work environment

question: The concept of 'off-the-job training'
options: Training delivered away from the workplace, often by external providers
Where decision-making authority is located in an organisation
Strikes, work-to-rule, go-slow and overtime bans

answer: Training delivered away from the workplace, often by external providers

answer: Theory X: employees dislike work; Theory Y: employees are self-motivated
